steps.title: Convert 129% greater to a growth factor
steps.content:
If something is 129% greater than a previous value, the new value equals the old value plus 129% of itself:
$$\text{New} = \text{Old} + 1.29 \cdot \text{Old} = \text{Old} \times (1 + 1.29) = \text{Old} \times 2.29$$
So every 6-hour period, the algae mass multiplies by 2.29.

steps.title: Write the exponential growth formula
steps.content:
For exponential growth with constant multiplicative factor per time period:
$$A = A_0 \cdot r^t$$
where $A_0$ is the initial mass, $r = 2.29$ per 6-hour period, and $t$ is the number of periods.

Since time is measured in hours and each period is 6 hours:
$$t = \frac{x}{6}$$
Therefore:
$$A = A_0(2.29)^{\frac{x}{6}}$$

steps.title: Find the initial value using the 18-hour data point
steps.content:
Given: $A = 613.90$ grams at $x = 18$ hours.

Substitute into the formula:
$$613.90 = A_0(2.29)^{\frac{18}{6}} = A_0(2.29)^3$$

Calculate $(2.29)^3 = 12.009$:
$$A_0 = \frac{613.90}{12.009} \approx 51.12$$

The equation is $A = 51.12(2.29)^{\frac{x}{6}}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
